---
title: '`r paste0(params$name," report")`'
subtitle: "Parameterized Typst PDF"
author: "Roy Francis"
date: last-modified
format:
  typst:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    fig-align: left

params:
  name: setosa
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
cat("## ",params$name)
```

## Table

The `r params$name` species is subsetted from the data.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-data
#| tbl-cap: !expr paste0("Data for ",params$name," species.")
iris_filtered <- subset(iris, iris$Species == params$name)
head(iris_filtered)
```

## Plots

```{r}
#| label: fig-scatterplot
#| fig-cap: !expr paste0("Scatterplot of ",params$name," species.")
ggplot(iris_filtered,aes(Sepal.Length,Petal.Length,col=Species))+
    geom_point()+
    labs(title=params$name)
```

## Image

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-species
#| fig-cap: !expr paste0("Photograph of ",params$name," species.")

imgs <- c(
    "setosa" = "assets/setosa.jpg",
    "versicolor" = "assets/versicolor.jpg",
    "virginica" = "assets/virginica.jpg"
)

knitr::include_graphics(imgs[[params$name]])
```

The first rows of the subsetted dataset are shown in @tbl-data. The relationship between Sepal.Length and Petal.Length is shown in @fig-scatterplot.
